description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The barrel is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by three prominent black metal hoops—one near the top, one at the center, and one near the base. The wood has a dark, weathered finish, showing subtle grain patterns and some minor scuff marks, suggesting age and use. The top of the barrel is closed with a flat, circular wooden lid that fits flush with the rim. There are no visible handles or spouts, and the surface appears solid and robust. The overall appearance is utilitarian, suitable for storage or decorative use in urban or rustic settings.
description: A dark, weathered wooden barrel with three black metal hoops and a flat wooden lid, showing signs of age and use. The surface is robust and slightly textured, with no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bulging center, three black metal hoops, and the flat wooden lid with visible wood grain.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the curvature of the staves and the seamless fit of the lid.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the symmetry of the barrel and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Another angled view showing the consistent wood texture and the solid construction of the barrel.
